message:
fix(core): correct reserved-bytes page-size handling across B-tree operations

Fix a fundamental correctness issue where FrankenSQLite used page_size
(full page) instead of usable_size (page_size minus reserved_per_page
bytes) for B-tree cell content areas. This caused incorrect traversal,
corrupt page initialization, and broken reads of C SQLite databases with
non-default page configurations (e.g., page_size=8192, reserve_bytes=32).

Connection layer:
- Rename current_btree_usable_size_in_txn → current_btree_cursor_sizes_in_txn
  returning (usable_size, page_size) tuple
- Add centralized cursor constructors: new_pager_btree_cursor,
  new_header_btree_cursor, new_header_btree_index_cursor,
  configure_btree_cursor_page_size
- Refactor ~15 call sites from inline BtCursor with page_size.get() to
  use helpers ensuring usable_size for B-tree, page_size set separately
- Fix allocate_root_page/allocate_index_root_page to use usable_size
- Change CompoundOp::Intersect|Except from unreachable!() to NotImplemented

Compat loader:
- Add load_sqlite_cursor_sizes_from_page1() parsing real database header
- New test: C SQLite DB with page_size=8192 + reserve_bytes=32

VDBE engine:
- Add BtreeCursorPageLayout struct with helpers to read page 1 header
- All storage cursor paths use usable_size for B-tree operations
- Fresh root pages set cell content offset to usable_size
- New test: zero-page honors page1 reserved bytes layout

Write coordinator:
- Store authoritative page_size from DB header at construction time
- infer_page_size() uses stored value as fallback (not hardcoded 4096)

E2E:
- New test: mixed read/write on C SQLite copy with 8192-byte pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
